author: kevaundray
content:
1) Yep the guest program will deserialize input data

2) The proof never gets ingested by the guest program unless I'm misunderstanding. Proof is ingested by the CL

3) Yep exactly -- I was proposing that we standardize after everyone explores what works for them, but also happy to go with ssz for now since it should be more efficient than rlp but less efficient than something like bincode in rust

author: kevaundray
content:
For this PR, we went with rlp because there is no ssz library in the execution specs: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2362#pullrequestreview-3877322130

Once this is merged, we will have a release and I can browse your code for the changes needed! If you already ssz the ExecutionPayload, then this is most of the work already done when we introduce ssz

author: 0xrubo
content: thanks, i'll check it out. meanwhile, we got [this](https://github.com/NethermindEth/nethermind/tree/feature/stateless-zisk/tools/Stateless#input-serialization) for now for the input serialization

author: kevaundray
content: Looks good -- in the specs, we remove keys btw

author: kevaundray
content: The keys may be added back in the future with the rationale that they allow us to do prestate checks before execution

author: kevaundray
content: btw I wrote this four weeks ago in a hackmd, it might help the mental modelling for this

author: kevaundray
content:
So theres the "stateless" part that can be ran on a normal machine and tested. This has nothing to do with zkEVM.

Then theres stateless being executed under a zkvm

author: kevaundray
content: Not saying I saw anything to warrant this, just wanted to share 😄
